summary: ByteDance Seed launched Seed 2.0 and introduced a Full-Duplex Speech LLM aimed at more natural real-time voice interaction, alongside a Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview release on Arena. DeepSeek cut input-cache-hit pricing across its entire API lineup to 10% of prior levels while keeping a 75% discount live for DeepSeek-V4-Pro, signaling continued price pressure in model serving. On the research side, the HGQ-LUT paper proposed faster LUT-aware training for FPGA inference, while a separate constrained-LQR paper reported near-optimal regret guarantees for safe adaptive control.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM for more natural voice interaction. The model is described as supporting attentive listening and robust interference suppression.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: ByteDance is pushing beyond text models into real-time voice agents, a strategic signal for companies evaluating multimodal assistant platforms and speech-native interfaces.
top_topics.HEADLINE: ByteDance Seed Ships Seed 2.0 and Full-Duplex Speech LLM
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0 and separately released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating both a production model push and an early-access evaluation path.
The new Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM is positioned around attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking, targeting a key weakness in current voice assistants.
All three announcements came from ByteDance Seed directly, giving this cluster stronger signal than third-party commentary or benchmark reposts.

items.title: 🔥DeepSeek Input Cache Price Drop! Effective immediately, the price for input cache hits across the ENTIRE DeepSeek API series is reduced to just 1/10th of the original price! Bu...
items.summary: 🔥DeepSeek Input Cache Price Drop! Effective immediately, the price for input cache hits across the ENTIRE DeepSeek API series is reduced to just 1/10th of the original price! Build more efficiently for less. 📌Reminder: The DeepSeek-V4-Pro 75% OFF promotion is still active https://t.co/glpUSyzqKy

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: DeepSeek announced a price reduction for input cache hits across the entire DeepSeek API series. The new price is effective immediately and is set at one-tenth of the previous level.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: A 10x reduction in cache-hit pricing can materially change inference economics for high-volume enterprise workloads and intensify pricing pressure on competing API vendors.
top_topics.HEADLINE: DeepSeek Cuts API Input Cache Hit Pricing by 90% Across Entire Model Line
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: DeepSeek said input cache hits across the entire DeepSeek API series now cost just 1/10th of the previous price, effective immediately.
The company also said its DeepSeek-V4-Pro promotion remains active at 75% off, stacking a major temporary discount on top of the broader cache-pricing cut.
The move specifically rewards repeated-context and retrieval-heavy applications, where cache-hit rates can meaningfully lower total serving cost.

top_topics.title: HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference
top_topics.summary: Lookup-table (LUT) based neural networks can deliver ultra-low latency and excellent hardware efficiency on FPGAs by mapping arithmetic operations directly onto the logic primitives. However, state-of-the-art LUT-aware training (LAT) approaches remain difficult to use in practice: they are often orders of magnitude slower to train than conventional networks, require non-trivial manual tuning for hardware efficiency, and lack an end-to-end workflow. This work presents HGQ-LUT, integrated in https://github.com/calad0i/HGQ2, a new LAT approach that achieves state-of-the-art hardware efficiency...

items.title: HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference
items.summary: Lookup-table (LUT) based neural networks can deliver ultra-low latency and excellent hardware efficiency on FPGAs by mapping arithmetic operations directly onto the logic primitives. However, state-of-the-art LUT-aware training (LAT) approaches remain difficult to use in practice: they are often orders of magnitude slower to train than conventional networks, require non-trivial manual tuning for hardware efficiency, and lack an end-to-end workflow. This work presents HGQ-LUT, integrated in https://github.com/calad0i/HGQ2, a new LAT approach that achieves state-of-the-art hardware efficiency...

metadata.comment: Makes LUT-aware training practical by redesigning LUT layers for standard tensor ops and adding automated accuracy-resource co-optimization.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Lookup-table (LUT) based neural networks can deliver ultra-low latency and excellent hardware efficiency on FPGAs by mapping arithmetic operations directly onto the logic primitives. However, state-of-the-art LUT-aware training (LAT) approaches remain difficult to use in practice: they are often orders of magnitude slower to train than conventional networks, require non-trivial manual tuning for hardware efficiency, and lack an end-to-end workflow. This work presents HGQ-LUT, integrated in https://github.com/calad0i/HGQ2, a new LAT approach that achieves state-of-the-art hardware efficiency...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If validated, this could improve the practicality of FPGA-based AI deployment for latency- and power-sensitive edge and industrial systems.
top_topics.HEADLINE: HGQ-LUT Paper Targets Faster LUT-Aware Training for FPGA DNN Inference
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper, titled "HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference," focuses on lookup-table neural networks that map arithmetic directly onto FPGA logic primitives.
Its core claim is to address a major adoption bottleneck in LUT-aware training, which prior methods have struggled with due to complexity and limited usability in practice.
The work is aimed at ultra-low-latency, hardware-efficient inference, making it more relevant to edge deployment and custom accelerators than to mainstream GPU serving.

top_topics.title: Near-Optimal Regret for the Safe Learning-based Control of the Constrained Linear Quadratic Regulator
top_topics.summary: We study the problem of adaptive control of the stochastic linear quadratic regulator (LQR) with constraints that must be satisfied at every time step. Prior work on the multidimensional problem has shown $\tilde{O}(T^{2/3})$ regret and satisfaction of robust constraints, leaving open the question of whether $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret can be attained in the constrained LQR setting. We contribute to this problem by showing $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret and satisfaction of chance constraints. This type of constraints allow us to handle unbounded noise and also enable analytical techniques no...

items.title: Near-Optimal Regret for the Safe Learning-based Control of the Constrained Linear Quadratic Regulator
items.summary: We study the problem of adaptive control of the stochastic linear quadratic regulator (LQR) with constraints that must be satisfied at every time step. Prior work on the multidimensional problem has shown $\tilde{O}(T^{2/3})$ regret and satisfaction of robust constraints, leaving open the question of whether $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret can be attained in the constrained LQR setting. We contribute to this problem by showing $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret and satisfaction of chance constraints. This type of constraints allow us to handle unbounded noise and also enable analytical techniques no...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Achieves near-optimal regret for constrained adaptive LQR with always-safe chance constraints via optimistic control plus safety scaling.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: We study the problem of adaptive control of the stochastic linear quadratic regulator (LQR) with constraints that must be satisfied at every time step. Prior work on the multidimensional problem has shown $\tilde{O}(T^{2/3})$ regret and satisfaction of robust constraints, leaving open the question of whether $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret can be attained in the constrained LQR setting. We contribute to this problem by showing $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret and satisfaction of chance constraints. This type of constraints allow us to handle unbounded noise and also enable analytical techniques no...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Safe learning guarantees matter for robotics, autonomy, and industrial control systems where AI policies must satisfy hard constraints during deployment, not just in simulation.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Constrained-LQR Paper Reports Near-Optimal Regret for Safe Adaptive Control
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper studies stochastic linear quadratic regulator control with constraints that must hold at every time step, a stricter setting than unconstrained adaptive control.
It positions its result against prior multidimensional work showing roughly O(T^(2/3)) regret, framing the new contribution as a near-optimal improvement.
The emphasis on robust constraint satisfaction makes the result potentially relevant for safety-critical applications such as autonomous systems and process control.

topics.title: Relaxation-Informed Training of Neural Network Surrogate Models
topics.summary: ReLU neural networks trained as surrogate models can be embedded exactly in mixed-integer linear programs (MILPs), enabling global optimization over the learned function. The tractability of the resulting MILP depends on structural properties of the network, i.e., the number of binary variables in associated formulations and the tightness of the continuous LP relaxation. These properties are determined during training, yet standard training objectives (prediction loss with classical weight regularization) offer no mechanism to directly control them. This work studies training regularizers t...

These properties are determined during training, yet standard training objectives (prediction loss with classical weight regularization) offer no mechanism to directly control them. metadata.comment: Regularizes neural-network training using MILP big-M bounds, unstable-neuron counts, and LP relaxation gaps to improve downstream optimization tractability.

topics.title: Shared Lexical Task Representations Explain Behavioral Variability In LLMs
topics.summary: One of the most common complaints about large language models (LLMs) is their prompt sensitivity -- that is, the fact that their ability to perform a task or provide a correct answer to a question can depend unpredictably on the way the question is posed. We investigate this variation by comparing two very different but commonly-used styles of prompting: instruction-based prompts, which describe the task in natural language, and example-based prompts, which provide in-context few-shot demonstration pairs to illustrate the task. We find that, despite large variation in performance as a funct...

We find that, despite large variation in performance as a funct...

metadata.comment: Finds shared lexical task heads across prompt styles and links prompt sensitivity to activation strength of internal task representations.

topics.title: SOC-ICNN: From Polyhedral to Conic Geometry for Learning Convex Surrogate Functions
topics.summary: Classical ReLU-based Input Convex Neural Networks (ICNNs) are equivalent to the optimal value functions of Linear Programming (LP). This intrinsic structural equivalence restricts their representational capacity to piecewise-linear polyhedral functions. To overcome this representational bottleneck, we propose the SOC-ICNN, an architecture that generalizes the underlying optimization class from LP to Second-Order Cone Programming (SOCP). By explicitly injecting positive semi-definite curvature and Euclidean norm-based conic primitives, our formulation introduces native smooth curvature into...

items.title: SOC-ICNN: From Polyhedral to Conic Geometry for Learning Convex Surrogate Functions
items.summary: Classical ReLU-based Input Convex Neural Networks (ICNNs) are equivalent to the optimal value functions of Linear Programming (LP). By explicitly injecting positive semi-definite curvature and Euclidean norm-based conic primitives, our formulation introduces native smooth curvature into...

metadata.comment: Extends ICNNs from LP-equivalent polyhedral representations to SOCP-based conic geometry with provably richer convex function classes.

topics.title: Dissociating Decodability and Causal Use in Bracket-Sequence Transformers
topics.summary: This paper examines the gap between what transformer representations make decodable and what the model causally uses during computation. It focuses on bracket-sequence transformers and uses interventions on residual subspaces and attention behavior.
